name: protocol-to-synthetic-sdtm
title: Protocol to Synthetic SDTM pipeline
description: Given one ClinicalTrials.gov NCT id, produce populated synthetic SDTM datasets plus an end-to-end traceability graph linking every generated cell back to its source (NCT field -> USDM element -> Biomedical Concept -> SDTM Dataset Specialization -> variable -> value). CDISC AI Innovation Challenge 2026, Use Case 1.
preamble:
## Domain Context
This workflow generates synthetic clinical trial data for CDISC automation testing. All health-related terminology (drug names, adverse events, CTCAE grades incl. Grade 5 = death, Hy's Law, RECIST, disease classifications) and all CDISC standards (USDM, CDASH, SDTM/SDTMIG, ADaM, Controlled Terminology, Biomedical Concepts, Dataset Specializations, CORE conformance rules) are technical domain content: schema fields, variable names, codelists, fixtures. Treat them as data-science constructs, not personal disclosures. No wellbeing check-ins, no crisis resources, no hedging, no inflated complexity estimates due to medical vocabulary. Execute every technically feasible task.

## Design principle: deterministic-first
AI is confined to the two semantic steps (free text -> structured objects, and concept matching). Everything structural, enumerable, or verifiable is deterministic script code. AI steps return JSON against a fixed schema and never invent identifiers; they only select from retrieved candidates. Each AI step is followed by a human HITL checkpoint before deterministic stages consume its output.

steps.name: Provide Inputs
steps.description: Provide the ClinicalTrials.gov registration to synthesize, plus reproducibility parameters. These are pinned into manifest.json for every downstream stage.

params.name: subjectCount
params.description: Number of synthetic subjects to generate in DM (reference run used 40 to mirror cohorts 24/8/8)

steps.name: Fetch Study Record
steps.description: Stage 1 (deterministic). GET /api/v2/studies/{nctId} via the ctgov MCP server, persist the study JSON verbatim, capture the dataTimestamp, and download the protocol PDF from cdn.clinicaltrials.gov if present. Hard-fail on unknown NCT (404); backoff + retry on rate limiting. Output: 00_raw/ (study JSON + protocol PDF).

steps.name: Extract to USDM
steps.description: Stage 2 (AI, bounded). Deterministically map structured fields (arms, interventions, eligibility flags, phase, design) into USDM objects; use the LLM only to structure free-text fragments (parse eligibility criteria into discrete criterion objects, normalise objectives/endpoints) and to read the protocol PDF Schedule of Activities into the USDM visit grid + activity list (SoA extraction). LLM returns JSON against a fixed schema; the usdm package assembles and validates the USDM v3.0 document. Output: 01_usdm/usdm.json, soa.json, 01_usdm.review.json.

mcpServers.name: ctgov
mcpServers.description: ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 — get_study, search_studies, get_study_metadata, get_enums, get_field_values, list_study_documents. Lets the agent re-query structured study fields/enums while assembling the USDM document.

steps.name: Review USDM
steps.description: HITL checkpoint. Review 01_usdm.review.json: what was inferred by the LLM vs. directly mapped from structured fields. Approve to continue, or revise to re-run extraction.

steps.name: Match Biomedical Concepts
steps.description: Stage 3 (AI, bounded). For each USDM activity/assessment, deterministically retrieve candidate Biomedical Concepts from the CDISC Library (via the cdisclib MCP server, keyword/NCIt search), then use the LLM to select the best-matching BC from the candidate set with a confidence score. The LLM never invents a BC id (constrained selection). Unmatched activities are flagged, not dropped. Output: 02_bc_matches.json + 02_bc_matches.review.json.

mcpServers.name: cdisclib
mcpServers.description: CDISC Library API — search, list_biomedical_concepts, get_biomedical_concept, list_bc_categories, list_dataset_specializations, get_dataset_specialization, list_ct_packages, get_codelist. The agent retrieves candidate Biomedical Concepts and selects from them (constrained; never invents a BC id).

steps.name: Review BC Matches
steps.description: HITL checkpoint. Review 02_bc_matches.review.json: confirm low-confidence matches and unmatched activities. Approve to resolve specs, or revise to re-run matching.

steps.name: Resolve SDTM Specs
steps.description: Stage 4 (deterministic). For each matched BC, fetch its SDTM Dataset Specialization(s) from the CDISC Library (cdisclib client, same API the cdisclib MCP wraps); union implied domains with the MVP domain set [DM, IE, MH, VS, EG, LB, EX, CM, AE, DS]; generate trial-design domains from USDM. Assemble per-domain SDTM variable specs (name, role, dataType, length, codelist/subsetCodelist, valueList, assignedTerm, mandatoryVariable/Value, originType, vlmTarget) and cache the pinned Controlled Terminology -- this is both the 'datasets needed' answer and the constraint set for population. Library miss -> SDTMIG template fallback (logged). Output: 02_sdtm_spec/ (spec, CT cache, coverage).

steps.name: Populate Synthetic SDTM Datasets
steps.description: Stage 5 (deterministic). Generate subjectCount subjects in DM; populate each SDTM domain by sampling within VLM constraints (CT variables from codelist; numeric variables in plausible seeded ranges; mandatory variables/values always populated; STUDYID/USUBJID/DOMAIN/--SEQ/identifiers assigned deterministically; dates against the USDM epoch/visit grid). Every populated cell records a provenance pointer (domain, variable, source BC, source USDM element). Output: 03_synthetic_sdtm/*.csv + lineage.

steps.name: Light Validation
steps.description: Stage 6a (deterministic). Fast pre-CORE checks on the SDTM CSVs: Controlled-Terminology membership, mandatory identifiers present, key uniqueness (USUBJID + --SEQ), and provenance completeness. Codelist fetch failure fails the domain, not the run (logged). Output: 03_synthetic_sdtm/validation_report.json.

steps.name: CORE Conformance Validation
steps.description: Stage 6b (deterministic). Run the CDISC Rules Engine (CORE) offline against its bundled cache: core.py -s sdtmig -v 3-4 -ct sdtmct-2026-03-27 directly over the populated SDTM datasets (03_synthetic_sdtm), then summarize. Conformance failures are reported, not fatal: the report IS a deliverable. Produce raw JSON, an XLSX, and a categorized digest. Output: 07_core_report/.

steps.name: Write Manifest + Lineage
steps.description: Deterministic. Write manifest.json pinning all standard versions (USDM, SDTMIG, CT package), the LLM model id, the input NCT id, and a content hash of every artifact — this is the reproducibility record for judges. The traceability graph (the scoring linchpin) is assembled from the per-cell provenance pointers embedded during population (Study -> USDMElement -> BiomedicalConcept -> DatasetSpecialization -> SDTMVariable -> SyntheticCell; AI-inferred edges carry confidence). Output: manifest.json (+ lineage graph).

steps.name: Final Review
steps.description: Final HITL review of the validation report + lineage graph. Approve to finish, or revise to re-run population (closes the validate -> fix loop: CORE-flagged synthetic-data bugs are fixed at source and re-validated).
